subject: Public Health
title: Eradicating Smallpox
description: Learn Eradicating Smallpox in one minute.

hook.text: Smallpox killed 300 million people in the 20th century alone—then humanity erased it completely.

buildup.text: Edward Jenner's cowpox vaccine in 1796 was the first step. Global campaigns began in the 1960s.

discovery.text: The WHO's ring vaccination strategy surrounded every outbreak, vaccinating all contacts.

twist.text: The last natural case occurred in Somalia in 1977. By 1980, the WHO declared smallpox eradicated.

climax.text: It remains the only human disease ever deliberately and completely eliminated from nature.

punchline.text: Humanity's greatest health victory: a disease erased forever.

quiz.question: When was smallpox officially declared eradicated?
